llm_response.comment:
By analyzing the compiler error message, the issue was a missing `#[trigger]` annotation in a quantifier assertion within `lemma_perm_swap`. Verus requires manual triggers for certain quantifiers to guide the SMT solver (Z3). I will add the necessary triggers to the assertions in the lemmas. I will also ensure that `lemma_perm_trans` has appropriate triggers for robustness.

Here is the corrected and complete Verus code:

raw.stderr:
note: automatically chose triggers for this expression:
  --> /scratch/gpfs/ARORA/haoyu/jiawei/algoveri/test_results/bubble_sort_eval_1765762825055.rs:82:20
   |
82 | ...   && (forall|i: int| 0 <= i < v1.len() ==> v2[i] == v1[p[i]]);
   |          ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^

note:   trigger 1 of 1:
  --> /scratch/gpfs/ARORA/haoyu/jiawei/algoveri/test_results/bubble_sort_eval_1765762825055.rs:82:58
   |
82 | ...   && (forall|i: int| 0 <= i < v1.len() ==> v2[i] == v1[p[i]]);
   |                                                ^^^^^

note: automatically chose triggers for this expression:
  --> /scratch/gpfs/ARORA/haoyu/jiawei/algoveri/test_results/bubble_sort_eval_1765762825055.rs:85:20
   |
85 | ...   && (forall|i: int| 0 <= i < v2.len() ==> v3[i] == v2[p[i]]);
   |          ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^

note:   trigger 1 of 1:
  --> /scratch/gpfs/ARORA/haoyu/jiawei/algoveri/test_results/bubble_sort_eval_1765762825055.rs:85:58
   |
85 | ...   && (forall|i: int| 0 <= i < v2.len() ==> v3[i] == v2[p[i]]);
   |                                                ^^^^^

note: Verus printed one or more automatically chosen quantifier triggers
      because it had low confidence in the chosen triggers.
      To suppress these messages, do one of the following:
        (1) manually annotate a single desired trigger using #[trigger]
            (example: forall|i: int, j: int| f(i) && #[trigger] g(i) && #[trigger] h(j)),
        (2) manually annotate multiple desired triggers using #![trigger ...]
            (example: forall|i: int| #![trigger f(i)] #![trigger g(i)] f(i) && g(i)),
        (3) accept the automatically chosen trigger using #![auto]
            (example: forall|i: int, j: int| #![auto] f(i) && g(i) && h(j))
        (4) use the --triggers-silent command-line option to suppress all printing of triggers.
      (Note: triggers are used by the underlying SMT theorem prover to instantiate quantifiers;
      the theorem prover instantiates a quantifier whenever some expression matches the
      pattern specified by one of the quantifier's triggers.)
  --> /scratch/gpfs/ARORA/haoyu/jiawei/algoveri/test_results/bubble_sort_eval_1765762825055.rs:85:20
   |
85 | ...   && (forall|i: int| 0 <= i < v2.len() ==> v3[i] == v2[p[i]]);
   |          ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
